purpose: Defines the `!p_track_plan [--dashboard {id}] {plan_path}` command — a plan-document-driven variant of !p_track. The master reads a pre-written .md plan (typically from !plan output), translates it FAITHFULLY (no redesign) into waves/tasks/dependencies, populates dashboard files, executes the NON-NEGOTIABLE approval gate, then runs the same Phase 2 (execution) and Phase 3 (completion+report) as !p_track. Plan source path is stored in task.plan_source. Same dashboard tracking thresholds apply (3+ agents or >1 wave = mandatory).
gotchas: Plan document is the SOURCE OF TRUTH — translate faithfully, never redesign during translation
Plan ambiguities/gaps must be SURFACED at the approval gate, not silently filled in
Plan document is READ-ONLY — the master never modifies the source plan
Approval gate is NON-NEGOTIABLE — write `permission` log entry FIRST, then ask in terminal, then HALT (no master_state.json, no Task tool calls until approved)
validate-plan-required.sh hook blocks initialization.json writes when plan.json is missing/invalid
validate-approval-gate.sh hook blocks worker dispatch until the user approves via a `permission` → `info "Approval granted"` log entry pair
Every task must trace back to a specific section of the source plan document — traceability requirement
task.plan_source MUST be added to initialization.json with the absolute path to the source plan
patterns: Three phases: Phase 0 Plan Ingestion (Steps 0A-0D parse+read+analyze+validate) → Phase 1 Translation (Steps 1-11 mirroring p_track planning) → Phase 2/3 Execution+Completion (delegated to p_track docs)
Translation rules: plan phases→waves, plan bullet points→tasks, phase ordering→dependencies, preserve plan structure, store plan source
Approval gate sequence: write permission log → present mapping → HALT → on approval log 'Approval granted — activating eager dispatch' at info level → proceed
conventions: Top-of-file NON-NEGOTIABLE RULES blockquote (5 numbered rules) — same pattern as p_track.md
Phases are short — most detail delegated to agent/_commands/p_track_planning.md, p_track_execution.md, p_track_completion.md (and to p_track.md itself for shared rules)
